gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/525)

It has been argued that al-Majlisi made an error while grading this chain because in the pathway to Ali ibn al-Hakam; Ahmad bin Muhammad is not Ahmad bin Muhammad bin 'Eesa (Reliable) but rather Ahmad bin Muhammad bin Sayyar (Very Weak, Exaggerator and Corrupt beliefs) which would result in the chain being graded as weak
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/525)

It has been argued that al-Majlisi made an error while grading this chain because in the pathway to Ali ibn al-Hakam; Ahmad bin Muhammad is not Ahmad bin Muhammad bin 'Eesa (Reliable) but rather Ahmad bin Muhammad bin Sayyar (Very Weak, Exaggerator and Corrupt beliefs) which would result in the chain being graded as weak
text: إِنَّ الْقُرْآنَ الَّذِي جَاءَ بِهِ جَبْرَئِيلُ ( عليه السلام ) إِلَى مُحَمَّدٍ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) سَبْعَةَ عَشَرَ أَلْفَ آيَةٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Hisham Bin Salim,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The Quran which Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> came with unto Muhammad<sup>saww</sup> was of seventeen thousand Verses’.
en.sarwar: 28. Ali ibn al-Hakam has narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘The Holy Quran that Jibril (Gabriel) brought to Muhammad (a.s.) had seventeen thousand verses.’”
